div.head: The Minister in China (MacMurray) to the Secretary of State

list.item: It has been the Legation’s understanding that the establishment of customary naval honors with the Chinese authorities would be resumed in connection with the ceremonies to be observed on the reoccupation of the Nanking consulate. The consulate was reoccupied on December 15th without any ceremonies whatsoever and the question of such formalities has not been otherwise disposed of Admiral Bristol has now inquired of me as to the attitude which he should adopt in this particular.
… I should however be glad to ascertain the Department’s view with regard to this matter in order that I may inform Admiral Bristol of the course to be followed.
